name: Sherman, Lee
resolved_employer: City of Austin employee; also self-employed engineer (per his own FEC contributor disclosure)
evidence: FEC individual-contribution records show a Lee Sherman at zip 78702 self-reporting employer/occupation as 'CITY OF AUSTIN AND SELF EMPLOYED TOO/ENGINEER' ($216 to ActBlue, 12/29/2019); the 78702 zip and East Austin location match the D1 filing exactly. His 153 federal donations with a fully Democratic lean are consistent with recurring small-dollar ActBlue giving rather than PAC-level activity — no contributions to pro-Israel (AIPAC/UDP/DMFI), liberal-Zionist (J Street), pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, gun-control, or defense-contractor PACs surfaced, and he is not in the TEC lobbyist registry. No bio, board seat, or leadership page exists for him beyond a generic austintexas.gov contact form with no role information.

name: Baker, Chris
resolved_employer: Fringe Consulting (owner/consultant); founder and former Executive Director of The Other Ones Foundation
evidence: This is the Other Ones Foundation founder, not a different Chris Baker: his Austin-area professional profile lists him as Owner at Fringe Consulting and describes growing an organization's annual operating budget from $0 to $11M over 8 years and pioneering a low-barrier work program for people experiencing homelessness — TOOF, which he co-founded in summer 2017 with Phil Leal. Public address records place a Chris Baker at Austin 78748, matching the filing zip; TOOF's team page now lists a different executive director, consistent with his transition to a 'Founder & Ambassador' role and independent consulting. Caution on the pipeline's fec_partisan_lean of 0.062 across 265 donations: no Republican or conservative federal giving could be tied to this individual, and 'Chris Baker' is a high-collision name, so that lean score should be treated as an aggregation artifact and NOT as evidence of Republican alignment. No TEC lobbyist registration; no pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, gun-control, or defense-contractor PAC ties found.

affiliations.role: Co-founder; former Executive Director (now Founder & Ambassador)
affiliations.snippet: The Other Ones Foundation was started in the summer of 2017 by Chris Baker and Phil Leal to bring their innovative ideas for addressing homelessness to life; the nonprofit provides humanitarian aid, case management, and extremely low-barrier work opportunities to people experiencing homelessness in Austin.

name: Erazo, Angelica
evidence: The filing lists an Angelica Erazo in Alameda, CA (94501) as a cybersecurity engineer at Workday, whose headquarters is in nearby Pleasanton, CA — geographically consistent. A LinkedIn profile (linkedin.com/in/angelicaerazo) titled 'Angelica Erazo - Workday' authored a post 'What I Learned From Great Leaders in Austin, Texas,' and a separate published Lone Star Parity Project profile describes an Austin-based Angelica Erazo in civic tech who serves as vice chair of the City of Austin Hispanic/Latino Quality of Life Resource Advisory Commission and on the boards of Earth Day Austin, The New Philanthropists, and an Austin cybersecurity nonprofit (https://www.lonestarparityproject.org/post/angelica-erazo-utilizing-technology-to-help-communities-mobilize/). Those Austin civic roles are NOT recorded as affiliations here because no public source links the Austin civic figure to the Alameda/Workday donor record — the state mismatch is unresolved and the two may be different people. No lobbyist registration; no PAC or advocacy tie on any side of the searched spectrums could be attributed to the donor. Employer-fallback classification only.

name: Burke, Cecelia
resolved_employer: Travis County Tax Assessor-Collector / county voter registrar (retired)
evidence: Travis County Archives' elections exhibit documents Cecelia Burke as Travis County Tax Assessor-Collector (pictured at a 1989 voter registration drive); she preceded Bruce Elfant, who took office in 2013. The donor's Austin 78731 residence and extremely heavy, near-uniformly Democratic federal giving pattern (533 contributions, partisan lean 0.999) are consistent with a retired countywide Democratic officeholder, but no source directly ties the 78731 donor record to the former officeholder, so identity is probable rather than confirmed. No TEC lobbyist registration; no PAC or advocacy-org tie on any side of the searched spectrums.

affiliations.role: Tax Assessor-Collector and county voter registrar (former)

She holds a BA in Government from UT Austin and previously worked in Texas public child protective services, which plausibly explains an Austin municipal donation. affiliations.org: NASW National PACE (Political Action for Candidate Election) Board of Trustees
affiliations.snippet: Hokanson was elected co-chair of the National Political Action for Candidate Election (PACE) Board of Trustees at the National Association of Social Workers; PACE supports candidates for public office who align with NASW's goals.

Elected chair of NASW-MA's PACE committee, which 'promotes policies and legislation that are consistent with the goals and needs of social workers... through endorsements and financial contributions to candidates for public office.'
During the 2024 election cycle, she co-chaired Social Workers for Harris/Walz, which organized national community outreach and voter mobilization phone banks.
She also serves on boards for Influencing Social Policy and her town's Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion Committee, plus the Commission on Disabilities and Pride Committee.

name: Kenny, Conor
resolved_employer: Capital A Housing — Principal (affordable/mixed-income housing developer)
evidence: Conor Kenny is a principal at Capital A Housing, an Austin affordable-housing development consultancy; the self-reported occupation 'affordable housing builder' and Austin 78723 (East Austin) location match. He chaired the City of Austin Planning Commission (served 2017-2021, stepping down as chair in Aug. 2020 to work with Fayez Kazi on affordable housing) and served on the City of Austin Design Commission 2016-2021. Earlier career: investigative researcher on money in politics for Public Citizen in Washington, DC, and editor of Congresspedia.org. No TEC lobbyist registration surfaced; no PAC or advocacy tie on the Israel/Palestine, oil & gas, gun, or defense spectrums.

affiliations.snippet: The top two leaders of the city's Planning Commission - Chair Conor Kenny and Vice Chair Fayez Kazi - stepped down from the high-profile city board on Aug. 25 and will begin working together at Kazi's firm on projects to bring more affordable housing to Austin.
Conor Kenny serves as Principal at Capital A Housing, a development consultant specializing in affordable housing in Austin.
He currently serves on the executive board of the Austin Housing Coalition.
